question_text: Let $X = ( X _ { 1 } , X _ { 2 } , X _ { 3 } ) ^ { T } \sim N _ { 3 } ( \mu , \Sigma )$ where $ \mu = \left( { \begin{array} { l } { 3 } \\ { 2 } \\ { 4 } \end{array} } \right) , \qquad \Sigma = \left( { \begin{array} { r r r } { 4 } & { 2 } & { 1 } \\ { 2 } & { 3 } & { 0 } \\ { 1 } & { 0 } & { 2 } \end{array} } \right) . $ Find $E [ X _ { 1 } \mid ( X _ { 2 } = 4 , X _ { 3 } = 7 ) ]$ (in integer).
solution:
We use the conditional expectation formula for the multivariate normal distribution:

 $ E [ X _ { 1 } \mid X _ { 2 } = x _ { 2 } , X _ { 3 } = x _ { 3 } ] = \mu _ { 1 } + \Sigma _ { 1 2 } \Sigma _ { 2 2 } ^ { - 1 } \left( { x _ { 2 } - \mu _ { 2 } } \right) . $ 

Step 1: Identify components.

Partition Σ as

 $ \Sigma = \left( \begin{array} { l l } { { \Sigma _ { 1 1 } } } & { { \Sigma _ { 1 2 } } } \\ { { \Sigma _ { 2 1 } } } & { { \Sigma _ { 2 2 } } } \end{array} \right) , $ 

where $\Sigma _ { 1 1 } = 4 , \Sigma _ { 1 2 } = ( 2 , 1 )$ , and

 $ \Sigma _ { 2 2 } = { \binom { 3 } { 0 } } \ 0 $ 

Then $\Sigma _ { 2 2 } ^ { - 1 } = { \binom { 1 / 3 \quad 0 } { 0 \quad 1 / 2 } }$

Step 2: Compute the conditional mean.

 $ \Sigma _ { 1 2 } \Sigma _ { 2 2 } ^ { - 1 } = ( 2 , 1 ) \binom { 1 / 3 } { 0 } \ 0 = ( 2 / 3 , 1 / 2 ) . $ 

Hence

 $ E [ X _ { 1 } \mid X _ { 2 } = x _ { 2 } , X _ { 3 } = x _ { 3 } ] = 3 + ( 2 / 3 ) ( x _ { 2 } - 2 ) + ( 1 / 2 ) ( x _ { 3 } - 4 ) . $ 

Substituting $x _ { 2 } = 4 , x _ { 3 } = 7$

 $ \begin{array} { r } { E [ X _ { 1 } \mid X _ { 2 } = 4 , X _ { 3 } = 7 ] = 3 + ( 2 / 3 ) ( 2 ) + ( 1 / 2 ) ( 3 ) = 3 + \frac { 4 } { 3 } + \frac { 3 } { 2 } = \frac { 6 + 4 + 4 . 5 } { 7 } = 3 + \frac { 1 7 } { 6 } = 5 . 8 3 \approx 6 . } \end{array} $ 

Thus, the required expectation is 6 .
Quick Tip

For partitioned multivariate normals, use $E [ X _ { 1 } \mid X _ { 2 } ] = \mu _ { 1 } + \Sigma _ { 1 2 } \Sigma _ { 2 2 } ^ { - 1 } ( x _ { 2 } - \mu _ { 2 } )$ . Ensure correct matrix partitioning and mean substitution.
